title: Caretaker Protocol — Component Longevity Framework
preview: Establish a repeatable, lightweight governance pattern for maintaining design tokens and core components (buttons, tables, charts) that balances stability for product teams with the ability to iterate. This ticket captures the proposed Caretaker Protocol: a definition of ownership, an RFC cadence for non-trivial cha...
text: Establish a repeatable, lightweight governance pattern for maintaining design tokens and core components (buttons, tables, charts) that balances stability for product teams with the ability to iterate. This ticket captures the proposed Caretaker Protocol: a definition of ownership, an RFC cadence for non-trivial changes, a compact review checklist, automated signals for component health, and a clear deprecation pathway.

title: Atomic component inventory, interaction-contrast playbook, and Figma tokens export
preview: Consolidate a single-source atomic component inventory and produce an interaction-contrast playbook that pairs with an exportable Figma token strategy. The goal is to remove ad-hoc variations in dashboard widgets, rollout rails, and cost cards; provide explicit contrast/hover/focus rules; and produce a clean tokens ...
text: Consolidate a single-source atomic component inventory and produce an interaction-contrast playbook that pairs with an exportable Figma token strategy. The goal is to remove ad-hoc variations in dashboard widgets, rollout rails, and cost cards; provide explicit contrast/hover/focus rules; and produce a clean tokens export that engineering can consume for console-2026.04 rollout.

title: Stackable component wrap + layout reflow anomalies on Safari/Firefox mobile — aggregation and prioritized QA
preview:
Problem statement:
Multiple regression reports from QA and customer support indicate that certain 'stackable' components (horizontal token stacks, responsive button groups, compact table rows, and chart legend chips) unexpectedly overflow, reflow, or collapse into a single-line on constrained viewports when used in ...
text:
Problem statement:
Multiple regression reports from QA and customer support indicate that certain 'stackable' components (horizontal token stacks, responsive button groups, compact table rows, and chart legend chips) unexpectedly overflow, reflow, or collapse into a single-line on constrained viewports when used in Safari on iOS and Firefox on Android. The behavior is inconsistent across breakpoints and sometimes linked to momentum scrolling or delayed font fallback. This ticket will centralize findings, record reliable repro steps, propose short-term mitigations, and create engineering tickets for long-term fixes.

Scope:
- Reproduce and classify failures into deterministic and timing-dependent categories.
- Produce minimal test cases (static HTML + component instances) for each category.
- Ship a short-lived CSS patch and a token spacing tweak for the next minor UI Kit release to re...

title: Global latency corridors — onboarding visual canvas & screenshot assets
preview: Create a set of onboarding/quickstart visuals and accompanying product screenshots that explain Redwood's region and latency routing behavior. Deliverables include an accessible global map canvas, a step-through animated storyboard (6 frames) for the quickstart tour, console screenshot overlays demonstrating routing...
text: Create a set of onboarding/quickstart visuals and accompanying product screenshots that explain Redwood's region and latency routing behavior. Deliverables include an accessible global map canvas, a step-through animated storyboard (6 frames) for the quickstart tour, console screenshot overlays demonstrating routing decisions, and export-ready SVG/PNG/MP4 assets for docs and product release notes.

title: Model Lineage Traffic Steward — compact wireflow for multi-model routing and rollback
preview: Goal: Prototype a compact rollout composer focused on multi-model traffic steering and per-variant lineage inspection. The flow should let engineers and PMs direct traffic across model variants (e.g., quantized / float32 / distilled), create conditional routing rules by request attributes, preview model lineage for ...
text: Goal: Prototype a compact rollout composer focused on multi-model traffic steering and per-variant lineage inspection. The flow should let engineers and PMs direct traffic across model variants (e.g., quantized / float32 / distilled), create conditional routing rules by request attributes, preview model lineage for sampled requests, and execute fast rollback at cohort or model-variant granularity. Emphasis: clear foreground actions for emergency snapback, explainable traces to audit which model served which sample, and a simulation mode that forecasts metric drift (latency, cost/unit, regression rate) before applying weights.

title: Component Evolution Guidelines: Tokens → Buttons → Tables → Charts
preview: Create an operational, lightweight playbook for evolving Redwood's design system components and tokens so product teams can contribute safely and platform teams can retire or modify variants without breaking apps. This covers authoring contributions (tokens, buttons, tables, charts), the review checklist for design ...
text: Create an operational, lightweight playbook for evolving Redwood's design system components and tokens so product teams can contribute safely and platform teams can retire or modify variants without breaking apps. This covers authoring contributions (tokens, buttons, tables, charts), the review checklist for design + engineering, a minimal RFC flow for large changes, and a pragmatic deprecation/retirement policy that balances migration effort with product stability.

title: Incident Worksheet: Structured Error Triage & Retry Heuristics for Console
preview: Design and prototype an Incident Worksheet canvas inside the Redwood Console that guides engineers through structured error triage for failed inference requests. The worksheet synthesizes telemetry, payload diffs, KV/cache hints, model variant fallbacks, and retry heuristics into a concise, actionable UI. Goal is to...
text: Design and prototype an Incident Worksheet canvas inside the Redwood Console that guides engineers through structured error triage for failed inference requests. The worksheet synthesizes telemetry, payload diffs, KV/cache hints, model variant fallbacks, and retry heuristics into a concise, actionable UI. Goal is to reduce mean time to resolution (MTTR) for failed request classes and make rollout rollback decisions (canary vs full) more confident. Target personas: SRE/ML Engineer on-call, Platform engineer running Dedicated clusters, and PM owning SLAs. Key screens: 1) Headline summary with failure taxonomy and cost delta; 2) Timeline + sequence map of retries & fallbacks; 3) Payload diff & ephemeral KV snapshot with redaction controls; 4) Retry rules visualizer with suggested playbook actions (backoff adjustments, model variant fallback, temporary routing); 5) Quick actions panel inc...

title: GPU Hub Minimap & Autoscale Gestalt — compact controls and triage overlay
preview:
Goal: Design a compact, high-density dashboard module (GPU Hub Minimap) for the Dedicated Capacity console that surfaces pool health, autoscaling posture, and rapid triage controls in a single glance. The module must work as a tiled card in the fleet overview and as a focused overlay for incident response.

Why this...
text:
Goal: Design a compact, high-density dashboard module (GPU Hub Minimap) for the Dedicated Capacity console that surfaces pool health, autoscaling posture, and rapid triage controls in a single glance. The module must work as a tiled card in the fleet overview and as a focused overlay for incident response.

Why this exists: Customers operating Dedicated GPU pools need a fast, low-noise way to detect emergent latency/cost regressions and take immediate mitigations (burst enable, drain, fallback route) without switching pages. Current fleet pages are too verbose for on-call flow; this ticket explores a condensed gestalt that preserves actionability.

Primary users: SREs, infra/platform engineers, customer success who perform triage or quick tuning on Dedicated pools.

Design constraints and guiding principles:
- Compactness: fit key signals into a 320x200 card while remaining readable o...

title: Secure Edge: first-run network canvas & CLI-first install visual assets
preview: Goal: Produce a cohesive set of onboarding visuals and product screenshots that support a CLI-first private deployment experience for Secure Edge (Redwood Private). This ticket covers: a network canvas storyboard for the operator flow, high-fidelity screenshots for the command-line installer and post-install Console...
text:
Goal: Produce a cohesive set of onboarding visuals and product screenshots that support a CLI-first private deployment experience for Secure Edge (Redwood Private). This ticket covers: a network canvas storyboard for the operator flow, high-fidelity screenshots for the command-line installer and post-install Console snapshots, an upgrade/rollback visual pattern for air-gapped appliance upgrades, and developer-facing microcopy for each step.

Scope and deliverables: see `assets` list. Visual direction: minimal, operator-focused, emphasize connectivity state and safe-fallback affordances. Illustrate network topology as editable nodes (VPN, NAT, peering) rather than static diagrams so engineers can map their infra during first-run. Avoid marketing-style hero images; favor contextual screenshots + annotated overlays for docs.

Out-of-scope: marketing hero visuals, full localization delive...

title: Miniature Request Lens: compact span timeline & correlation scroller
preview: Goal: Design a compact, embeddable 'Miniature Request Lens' that fits into the Console request list and rollout detail panels for fast triage of problematic requests without navigating to the full trace inspector. The component should surface the most actionable information (critical spans, latency hotspots, correla...
text:
Goal: Design a compact, embeddable 'Miniature Request Lens' that fits into the Console request list and rollout detail panels for fast triage of problematic requests without navigating to the full trace inspector. The component should surface the most actionable information (critical spans, latency hotspots, correlation IDs, related traces) while preserving alignment with the Console's visual language and accessibility standards.

User problems we solve:
- Engineers and on-call SREs need a lightweight view to triage slow requests inline (list rows, rollout preview) without waiting for the heavy trace canvas to load.
- Product managers want a concise representation to compare pre/post-rollout performance in A/B rows.
- Designers need a consistent compact UI pattern to reuse across dashboards, alerts, and the rollout audit stream.

Design constraints and principles:
- Minimum footprint:...

title: MiniCoach — inline micro-actions for cost literacy during rollout setup
preview: Problem: users configuring model rollouts and capacity commits often lack quick, contextual guidance about the immediate cost impact of toggles (batching, KV cache, quantization). Large doc-driven onboarding interrupts flow. Goal: design a compact, dismissible "MiniCoach" micro-overlay that surfaces a single actiona...
text: Problem: users configuring model rollouts and capacity commits often lack quick, contextual guidance about the immediate cost impact of toggles (batching, KV cache, quantization). Large doc-driven onboarding interrupts flow. Goal: design a compact, dismissible "MiniCoach" micro-overlay that surfaces a single actionable tip tied to the control the user is interacting with, with a one-tap action (apply suggestion) and a link to deeper docs. The component should be small, non-modal, accessible, localizable, and trackable for analytics.

title: Audit Lens: operator access mapping & temporal grant explorer prototype
preview: Goal: Design a compact, searchable Audit Lens component for the Redwood Console that surfaces who has access to a resource, why they have it (role, group, direct grant, inherited), and how long that access lasts. Scope includes: workspace/org role mapping, temporal grants visualization, one-click access simulation, ...
text: Goal: Design a compact, searchable Audit Lens component for the Redwood Console that surfaces who has access to a resource, why they have it (role, group, direct grant, inherited), and how long that access lasts. Scope includes: workspace/org role mapping, temporal grants visualization, one-click access simulation, and an exportable incident snapshot for audits. This ticket covers the low-fidelity to interactive prototype, engineering handoff artifacts, and a first round of moderated usability tests with platform engineers and security reviewers.

title: Glassweight token harmonization for mixed-brand consoles and high-density table visuals in low-light mode
preview: Background: Multiple brand integrations and a recent push toward translucent "glass" surfaces have exposed inconsistencies in dark-mode token mappings. High-density table rows, compact controls, and chart stroke/area visibility suffer when brand accent intensity collides with contrast guardrails. This ticket harmoni...
text:
Background: Multiple brand integrations and a recent push toward translucent "glass" surfaces have exposed inconsistencies in dark-mode token mappings. High-density table rows, compact controls, and chart stroke/area visibility suffer when brand accent intensity collides with contrast guardrails. This ticket harmonizes a small, pragmatic token set ("glassweight" family) and defines component-level overrides so partners can opt into brand intensity without breaking accessibility or telemetry dashboards.

Goals:
- Define a concise glassweight token family for dark themes that controls surface translucency, tint strength, blur/opacity trade-offs, and micro-elevation for compact components.
- Map existing brand-accent tokens to conservative dark-mode fallbacks with quantized strength levels (low/medium/high) for predictable behavior.
- Introduce per-component override rules for tables, ch...

title: First-run optimizer visual suite: infographics, screenshot atlas, and microcopy for batch/cache/quant
preview: Design a cohesive, first-run visual suite to surface optimizer recommendations (batching, KV prefix caching, and quantization) in the product onboarding and docs. Deliverables: 1 hero interpretive infographic, a screenshot atlas with 6 annotated frames (desktop + mobile variants), concise in-product microcopy for ea...
text: Design a cohesive, first-run visual suite to surface optimizer recommendations (batching, KV prefix caching, and quantization) in the product onboarding and docs. Deliverables: 1 hero interpretive infographic, a screenshot atlas with 6 annotated frames (desktop + mobile variants), concise in-product microcopy for each recommendation, 3 explainer GIFs demonstrating batching and cache effects, and an export-ready asset pack (SVG, PNG@2x, source Figma file). Focus on clarity for engineers while retaining product-brand voice.

title: Token Economics Playbook — Cost Attribution & Export Patterns
preview: Design a reusable Token Economics Playbook UI kit for the Redwood Console that standardizes token-level cost breakdowns, attribution views, and export workflows. The goal is a set of composable patterns that product and engineering can reuse across Dashboards, Billing exports, and Rollouts dashboards to enable fast ...
text: Design a reusable Token Economics Playbook UI kit for the Redwood Console that standardizes token-level cost breakdowns, attribution views, and export workflows. The goal is a set of composable patterns that product and engineering can reuse across Dashboards, Billing exports, and Rollouts dashboards to enable fast diagnosis of per-token cost drivers and actionable pricing recommendations. This ticket delivers: 1) a primary cost-breakdown panel (token buckets, model variant, quantization, cache hit), 2) an attribution timeline (session-level and route-level aggregation), 3) CSV/JSON export mappings and preview, 4) pricing pattern components (rate-band sliders, cost-slab previews, SKU-aware labels), and 5) accessibility and performance constraints for large tenant datasets.

title: Orchestrated Performance Playbook: Route + Model Correlation UI and Chart Specs
preview: Design a guided performance investigation surface (the 'Performance Playbook') that combines per-route and per-model telemetry into correlated, interactive layers. The Playbook is intended for on-call engineers and SREs as well as product owners to quickly identify whether an incident is routing-, model-, or workloa...
text: Design a guided performance investigation surface (the 'Performance Playbook') that combines per-route and per-model telemetry into correlated, interactive layers. The Playbook is intended for on-call engineers and SREs as well as product owners to quickly identify whether an incident is routing-, model-, or workload-related and to take safe rollout actions. This ticket covers high-level UX flows, chart-level specs for three canonical correlation views, prototype links, and notes for handoff to frontend and infra for data requirements.

title: Token fingerprinting harness for per-model throughput + p95 regression detection
preview: Create and maintain a reproducible microbenchmark harness that fingerprints tokens/sec and p95 latency for our core served model variants (float32, int8/quantized, and compiled-kernel builds). This harness will be the canonical source-of-truth for per-model throughput baselines used by CI, rollout decisions, and bil...
text: Create and maintain a reproducible microbenchmark harness that fingerprints tokens/sec and p95 latency for our core served model variants (float32, int8/quantized, and compiled-kernel builds). This harness will be the canonical source-of-truth for per-model throughput baselines used by CI, rollout decisions, and billing guidance. It must exercise streaming & non-streaming paths, warm KV cache behavior, and sequence-length sensitivity.

title: Tokenwise requant budgeter and kernel provenance harness for dovetailed attention scheduling
preview: Problem: Mixed-precision pipelines (INT8/FP8) introduce per-token dequant/requant overhead that is disproportionately paid during prefill and early decode windows, increasing P95 latency and token cost for short-turn conversations. Current kernel selection lacks fine-grained token-aware budgeting and provenance tele...
text:
Problem: Mixed-precision pipelines (INT8/FP8) introduce per-token dequant/requant overhead that is disproportionately paid during prefill and early decode windows, increasing P95 latency and token cost for short-turn conversations. Current kernel selection lacks fine-grained token-aware budgeting and provenance telemetry, making it hard to decide when to incur a dequant vs. pick a slower FP32/FP16 kernel.

Proposal: Implement a "tokenwise requant budgeter" in the runtime scheduler that tracks a sliding budget across the active token window and decides, per token/head-group, whether to route to an INT8 fastpath with a requantization at the epilogue or to a safer FP8/FP16 kernel. Complement this with a "kernel provenance harness": lightweight telemetry attached to each kernel decision that records the features used to decide the path (sequence length, kv-hit ratio, headwise entropy, bat...

title: Cycle Pulse & Echo: Capacity Canvas and Slate Shepherding
preview: Problem: In high-velocity cycles we repeatedly hit two friction points: (1) teams over-commit early and then scramble to de-scope mid-cycle, and (2) platform ops can't reliably forecast burst capacity needs for Dedicated on short notice. Scope: establish a repeatable lightweight process and artifacts (Capacity Canva...
text: Problem: In high-velocity cycles we repeatedly hit two friction points: (1) teams over-commit early and then scramble to de-scope mid-cycle, and (2) platform ops can't reliably forecast burst capacity needs for Dedicated on short notice. Scope: establish a repeatable lightweight process and artifacts (Capacity Canvas + Slate Shepherd) that combine quantitative capacity budgeting with qualitative tradeoff surfaces so PMs and Eng leads can make deterministic cycle choices. Goals: reduce mid-cycle scope churn by 30%, shorten decision turnaround for staffing reallocations to <48h, and provide a 2-week predictive window for Dedicated capacity requests. Approach: introduce "Pulse" checkpoints (pre-cycle, week2 pulse, mid-cycle pulse) with clear signals and an "Echo" reserve policy (micro-credits pool + shadow roster). Deliverables: (1) Capacity Canvas template (workload-weighted token-hours...

title: Introduce expressive-outline variant and compact icon-stack button family
preview: Motivation: recent product requests and QA reports show inconsistent outline contrast across dark/high-contrast themes, and several surface-level regressions where loading spinners overlay icons incorrectly on compact icon buttons. This ticket introduces a new 'expressive-outline' variant (higher stroke contrast + s...
text: Motivation: recent product requests and QA reports show inconsistent outline contrast across dark/high-contrast themes, and several surface-level regressions where loading spinners overlay icons incorrectly on compact icon buttons. This ticket introduces a new 'expressive-outline' variant (higher stroke contrast + slightly heavier on hover) and a compact 'icon-stack' family that allows stacked primary/icon combinations for dense rows and mobile lists. Work includes token mapping, visual specs for light/dark/high-contrast, updated loading rules (spinner -> inline skeleton or replace icon), Storybook stories, and accessibility verification.

title: Launchpad mini-hero storyboard and interactive step tiles
preview: Design an alternative, compact hero for the Quickstart landing page that tells a concise onboarding story and surfaces interactive step tiles. The goal is to reduce initial vertical real estate while increasing discoverability of the next steps. Deliverables: hero storyboard (3 variants), micro-animation specs, inte...
text: Design an alternative, compact hero for the Quickstart landing page that tells a concise onboarding story and surfaces interactive step tiles. The goal is to reduce initial vertical real estate while increasing discoverability of the next steps. Deliverables: hero storyboard (3 variants), micro-animation specs, interactive step-tile component (desktop & mobile), screenshot export templates and a lightweight asset package for front-end implementation.

title: Cost Sentinel: Continuous Workload Profiling and Safe Batch Parameter Synthesizer
preview: Deliver a service component (Cost Sentinel) that continuously profiles production request streams to synthesize safe batching parameters and caching heuristics. The synthesizer will propose incremental config updates that can be applied automatically under conservative safety checks, and provide an automatic rollbac...
text: Deliver a service component (Cost Sentinel) that continuously profiles production request streams to synthesize safe batching parameters and caching heuristics. The synthesizer will propose incremental config updates that can be applied automatically under conservative safety checks, and provide an automatic rollback path when SLOs regress or error rates increase. This ticket scopes the initial engine, telemetry contracts, canary rollout plan, and integration points with the Console rollout APIs and the autoscaling subsystem.

title: Credential orchestration lab: visual assets for API key scoping, streaming & tool-calls
preview: Design and deliver a compact 'Credential Orchestration Lab' asset suite for the API/SDK quickstart. Assets should teach developers how to: create scoped API keys, enable streaming, and configure tool-calling permissions. Deliverables include static screenshots for docs, an animated micro-demo GIF (streaming token li...
text: Design and deliver a compact 'Credential Orchestration Lab' asset suite for the API/SDK quickstart. Assets should teach developers how to: create scoped API keys, enable streaming, and configure tool-calling permissions. Deliverables include static screenshots for docs, an animated micro-demo GIF (streaming token lifecycle), an accessible SVG sequence for key scoping, and code snippet overlays for JS/Python SDKs. Goal: reduce time-to-first-success by making auth and permission concepts explicit with visuals that can be embedded in docs and in-console help.

title: Pulse Inspector: GPU pool health timeline, autoscale tuning & burst policy editor
preview: Goal: Create a compact, operator-focused inspector panel (codename: Pulse) for Dedicated GPU pools that merges health timeline, topology status, autoscaling controls, and a burst policy editor into a single slide-over UI. The inspector must be glanceable for on-call engineers and also provide safe editing flows for ...
text:
Goal: Create a compact, operator-focused inspector panel (codename: Pulse) for Dedicated GPU pools that merges health timeline, topology status, autoscaling controls, and a burst policy editor into a single slide-over UI. The inspector must be glanceable for on-call engineers and also provide safe editing flows for SREs and platform owners.

Scope: initial design covers single-pool view (no multi-select), timeline-based health overview, recent incidents, autoscale target tuning (min/target/max + responsive knobs), burst budget editor, cost impact preview, simple rollback/canary controls, and contextual links to detailed pool logs and metrics dashboards.

Out of scope: full cluster topology management, user-level access controls (handled elsewhere), and deep cost forecasting (only an immediate per-hour estimate shown).

title: Launchpad empty-state explainer cards and guided microflows
preview: Design a compact, context-aware set of explainer cards and short guided microflows to populate the Redwood Console launchpad (home/dashboard) for new and low-activity users. The goal is to reduce time-to-first-success for new teams, surface high-impact actions (create API key, deploy model, view cost), and provide l...
text: Design a compact, context-aware set of explainer cards and short guided microflows to populate the Redwood Console launchpad (home/dashboard) for new and low-activity users. The goal is to reduce time-to-first-success for new teams, surface high-impact actions (create API key, deploy model, view cost), and provide lightweight discoverability without modal fatigue. This ticket covers patterns, visual language, microcopy, prototype handoff, and a lightweight A/B test plan for 2 variants.

title: Lighthouse: Request Trace Lab for Progressive Error Triaging
preview: Build a lightweight, exploratory design prototype called 'Lighthouse Request Trace Lab' that surfaces progressive breadcrumbs, retry lineage, and payload delta overlays to accelerate root-cause triage for failed requests in the Console. This is a designer-led prototype to validate interaction patterns for: 1) correl...
text: Build a lightweight, exploratory design prototype called 'Lighthouse Request Trace Lab' that surfaces progressive breadcrumbs, retry lineage, and payload delta overlays to accelerate root-cause triage for failed requests in the Console. This is a designer-led prototype to validate interaction patterns for: 1) correlating multi-attempt request timelines, 2) surfacing minimal payload diffs across retries, 3) presenting a compact error taxonomy with suggested remediation steps, and 4) integrating short-lived hypothesis runs (replay-with-override) to test fixes. The prototype should emphasize scanability, signal-to-noise controls, and easy handoff to Eng/PM for implementation planning.

title: Workspace Sentinel — modular sidebar, route scaffolding, and cross-workspace routing
preview: Design a modular sidebar and routing scaffold to support fast workspace switching, persistent dashboard anchors (cost, rollouts, model telemetry), and progressive disclosure for teams with multiple orgs. Focus on tactile affordances for quick context changes, keyboard-first flows, and clear signal for canary vs stab...
text: Design a modular sidebar and routing scaffold to support fast workspace switching, persistent dashboard anchors (cost, rollouts, model telemetry), and progressive disclosure for teams with multiple orgs. Focus on tactile affordances for quick context changes, keyboard-first flows, and clear signal for canary vs stable rollouts. Deliver prototypes for desktop and narrow / collapsed variants, annotated handoff specs, and an accessibility review.

title: Align typography, spacing, and color ramps for charts and tables
preview: Purpose: Reduce visual drift across data-heavy components (tables, charts, cards) by consolidating color ramps, tightening the typography scale at small sizes, and standardizing spacing and corner radii. Motivation: recent QA and customer feedback called out inconsistent row padding, low-contrast legends in charts, ...
text:
Purpose: Reduce visual drift across data-heavy components (tables, charts, cards) by consolidating color ramps, tightening the typography scale at small sizes, and standardizing spacing and corner radii. Motivation: recent QA and customer feedback called out inconsistent row padding, low-contrast legends in charts, and varied shadow strength between card components. This ticket scopes token changes (color, type, spacing, radius, elevation), the compatibility strategy, and delivery plan for the March DS release.

Proposed changes (high level):
- Color: introduce a unified neutral ramp for data surfaces (neutral-050..neutral-900) and a separate semantic ramp for data accents (accent-050..accent-700). Increase contrast for legend/text-on-data to meet AA for 12px minimum sizes. Update primary/secondary swatches used in charts to reduce vibrancy clashes.
- Typography: tighten small-size sc...

title: Quickstart insight-tile montage: latency / token-cost / traceflow screenshots
preview: Create a compact, production-ready set of onboarding/quickstart UX assets that demonstrate Redwood Console observability: latency distribution tiles, cost-per-token breakdown, and an annotated traceflow view. These assets will be used in the Quickstart docs, in-product first-time experience, and release notes. Deliv...
text: Create a compact, production-ready set of onboarding/quickstart UX assets that demonstrate Redwood Console observability: latency distribution tiles, cost-per-token breakdown, and an annotated traceflow view. These assets will be used in the Quickstart docs, in-product first-time experience, and release notes. Deliver a set of responsive screenshots, a montage hero for the docs landing page, and a short animated GIF showing trace zoom+annotation. Prioritize clarity for new users: highlight median vs. p95 latency, identify cost hotspots (model vs. tokens vs. cache hits), and show how traceflow surfaces root causes (kernel stalls, batch wait).

title: LandingPad sprint allocator and release-train governance
preview: Problem statement: As multiple feature teams target overlapping launch windows across runtime, console, and API surfaces, we lack a single, enforceable runway allocator that ties launch dates to capacity reservations, OKR deadlines, and rollback-safe guardrails. This creates last-minute contention for Dedicated capa...
text:
Problem statement: As multiple feature teams target overlapping launch windows across runtime, console, and API surfaces, we lack a single, enforceable runway allocator that ties launch dates to capacity reservations, OKR deadlines, and rollback-safe guardrails. This creates last-minute contention for Dedicated capacity, elevated latency risk, and misaligned communications for enterprise customers.

Goal: Build the "LandingPad" sprint allocator — a lightweight governance pattern and supporting coordination checklist that: (1) maps launches to capacity lanes (baseline, hedge, emergency), (2) enforces gating criteria per surface (runtime/console/api), (3) publishes a one-page cross-team runway with rollout owners and rollback triggers, and (4) integrates with quarterly OKRs and the release calendar so product planning and platform capacity are aligned.

Scope and deliverables:
- Define ...

title: normalize-variant-axes-and-auto-layout-prune-for-data-cards
preview: Objective: Clean up the Data Card component family in the Figma library to reduce variant combinatorial bloat, enforce predictable auto-layout behavior inside dense table/cell contexts, and align naming to the semantic token taxonomy. Scope: - Audit all Data Card instances used in Tables, Charts, and dense list view...
text: Objective: Clean up the Data Card component family in the Figma library to reduce variant combinatorial bloat, enforce predictable auto-layout behavior inside dense table/cell contexts, and align naming to the semantic token taxonomy. Scope: - Audit all Data Card instances used in Tables, Charts, and dense list views. - Consolidate variant axes (size / density / state) into a minimal contract that supports: compact, regular, interactive, disabled. - Remove deprecated nested auto-layout frames that cause collapse/flicker when constraints change. - Update token references (spacing, type, color) to use semantic tokens instead of hard-coded values. Out of scope: visual redesigns or feature additions to Data Card content. This ticket focuses on library hygiene and publish readiness.

title: Traffic Inspector & Explainable Rollback microflow — prototype and review
preview: Design a compact, developer-friendly Traffic Inspector panel and an ‘Explainable Rollback’ microflow for the Redwood Console rollouts experience. The goal is to give engineers and PMs a way to: 1) quickly inspect live traffic splits and recent decisioning (why traffic moved to variant B), 2) view the event timeline ...
text: Design a compact, developer-friendly Traffic Inspector panel and an ‘Explainable Rollback’ microflow for the Redwood Console rollouts experience. The goal is to give engineers and PMs a way to: 1) quickly inspect live traffic splits and recent decisioning (why traffic moved to variant B), 2) view the event timeline (errors, latency spikes, eval regressions) correlated with traffic changes, and 3) initiate a rollback with an explanation assistant that recommends rollback scope (full, partial, or target segment) and required follow-ups. This ticket covers low-fidelity flows, an interactive Figma prototype for usability testing with two SREs, and a short design review with Eng to align on telemetry landmarks we must surface in the UI.

title: Resilient mini-spark canvas: semantic prioritization, selective-announcer, and GPU-fallback strategy
preview: Replace current mini-sparkline rendering for dense time-series with a resilient canvas-first implementation that prioritizes semantic content for screen readers and implements a compact tooltip eviction policy to reduce CPU main-thread pressure. Deliver a pattern that gracefully falls back to CPU-lit SVG rendering w...
text: Replace current mini-sparkline rendering for dense time-series with a resilient canvas-first implementation that prioritizes semantic content for screen readers and implements a compact tooltip eviction policy to reduce CPU main-thread pressure. Deliver a pattern that gracefully falls back to CPU-lit SVG rendering when GPU/OffscreenCanvas is not available or when memory pressure crosses a deterministic threshold.

title: Horizon-band virtualization for long-running series & an a11y-first legend summarizer
preview: Problem: Time-series charts used in telemetry dashboards commonly display days-to-months of data. Rendering full-resolution lines for very long series kills interactivity and memory, and the legend becomes unusable for dozens of series. We need a design that maintains readable semantics for screen readers, preserves...
text: Problem: Time-series charts used in telemetry dashboards commonly display days-to-months of data. Rendering full-resolution lines for very long series kills interactivity and memory, and the legend becomes unusable for dozens of series. We need a design that maintains readable semantics for screen readers, preserves developer-configurable color tokens, and yields a progressive, performant render path across devices.

title: Compact aggregate announcer + render-decimation for high-density time-series
preview:
Problem:
High-density time-series views (dashboards with many small series or very long histories) are causing two user-facing issues: 1) screen-reader consumers get overwhelmed by thousands of live announcements when the chart updates, and 2) interactive latency spikes (hover/tooltip + legend updates) as DOM nodes ...
text:
Problem:
High-density time-series views (dashboards with many small series or very long histories) are causing two user-facing issues: 1) screen-reader consumers get overwhelmed by thousands of live announcements when the chart updates, and 2) interactive latency spikes (hover/tooltip + legend updates) as DOM nodes and per-series layers multiply.

Goal:
Design a lightweight, accessible pattern and visual affordance that: 1) collapses non-essential series into an ARIA-friendly summary that screen readers can consume without noise, 2) preserves discoverability and keyboard navigation for power users, and 3) reduces DOM and paint overhead to improve interactive latency for common dashboard workloads.

Constraints:
- Must be implementable without breaking existing SDK consumers of chart components.
- Must respect tokenized color and spacing system (so theme changes cascade).
- Should prov...

title: Attention kernel choosing safety-net and synthetic benchmark simulator
preview: Problem: We continue to see intermittent latency regressions and memory spikes when the kernel selector flips between FlashAttention v2 and v3 at certain batch/sequence-shape combinations during prefill and decode. Root cause is unclear across cases: some relate to v3 kernel workspace fragmentation, others to startu...
text:
Problem: We continue to see intermittent latency regressions and memory spikes when the kernel selector flips between FlashAttention v2 and v3 at certain batch/sequence-shape combinations during prefill and decode. Root cause is unclear across cases: some relate to v3 kernel workspace fragmentation, others to startup jitter when speculative warmup probes run.

Goal: Build a synthetic benchmark + simulator that can: 1) reliably reproduce the shape/occupancy conditions that trigger regressions, 2) exercise the kernel selection logic (including hysteresis, gating, and fallback), and 3) produce a set of safety thresholds and automatic rollback triggers for staged rollouts. The simulator should be lightweight, deterministic for CI, and capable of running on CI/GHA runners and on an engineering GPU pool.

Scope: implement workload generator, kernel selector harness (pluggable for v2/v3/quan...

title: Workspace Spotlight & Contextual Surface: navigation prototype for dashboards, rollouts, cost views
preview: Problem: The Console navigation is fragmented for users who need quick access to operational surfaces (dashboards, rollouts, cost breakdowns) across multiple workspaces. Current left-rail mixes global product routes with workspace-scoped pages leading to routing ambiguity and slower task completion. Goal: Design a c...
text: Problem: The Console navigation is fragmented for users who need quick access to operational surfaces (dashboards, rollouts, cost breakdowns) across multiple workspaces. Current left-rail mixes global product routes with workspace-scoped pages leading to routing ambiguity and slower task completion. Goal: Design a compact, discoverable navigation pattern that surfaces workspace-scoped destinations (dashboards, rollouts, cost views) without increasing cognitive load for occasional users, and that supports fast workspace switching for power users.

title: Dynamic SLI onboarding and traffic escrow policy for prioritized conversational endpoints
preview: Background: As we onboard more conversational routes and tuned model variants, teams need a predictable way to declare SLIs and safely migrate traffic without risking noisy error-budget burn across regions. This ticket defines a prescriptive onboarding flow that (1) creates per-model SLIs from sampled traffic and sy...
text:
Background: As we onboard more conversational routes and tuned model variants, teams need a predictable way to declare SLIs and safely migrate traffic without risking noisy error-budget burn across regions. This ticket defines a prescriptive onboarding flow that (1) creates per-model SLIs from sampled traffic and synthetic probe signals, (2) allocates an "traffic escrow" (error-budget currency) to new model variants, and (3) wires an automated regional failover choreography tied to the escrow balance and circuit-breaker state. The goal is to reduce rollouts that unexpectedly exhaust global error budgets and to enable short-lived experiments with automatic safe ejection.

Scope:
- Define canonical SLI signals for conversational endpoints (latency P95/P99, request-quality pass rate, token-error-rate, downstream HTTP error rate).
- Specify sampling rates and synthetic probe cadence for i...

title: Span Heatmap & Correlation Explorer for Request Drilldown
preview: Design a compact, scannable request drilldown view that surfaces span-level heatmaps, correlation IDs, and timeline analytics to help engineers quickly identify tail-latency contributors, model-variant fallbacks, and retry patterns. This explores a dual-pane layout: a left pane for request metadata + filters and a r...
text: Design a compact, scannable request drilldown view that surfaces span-level heatmaps, correlation IDs, and timeline analytics to help engineers quickly identify tail-latency contributors, model-variant fallbacks, and retry patterns. This explores a dual-pane layout: a left pane for request metadata + filters and a right pane for an interactive waterfall with a span heatmap overlay and resource usage annotations.

title: Platform interaction anomalies and token regression review for responsive UI kit
preview:
Goal: Run a focused cross-platform QA pass on the UI kit to identify responsive and cross-browser regressions introduced by recent token and component changes (tokens v2 merge + button polymorphism refactor).

Scope: buttons, interactive states, table headers/sticky behavior, responsive table collapse, chart renderi...
text:
Goal: Run a focused cross-platform QA pass on the UI kit to identify responsive and cross-browser regressions introduced by recent token and component changes (tokens v2 merge + button polymorphism refactor).

Scope: buttons, interactive states, table headers/sticky behavior, responsive table collapse, chart rendering at tight widths, and global token fallbacks (color, spacing, radius). Test matrix covers Desktop (Chrome/Firefox/Safari latest), iOS Safari, Android Chrome, and viewport breakpoints for mobile/portrait/landscape.

Deliverables: concise triage list with reproducible steps, prioritized ENG tickets for fixes, design patches (token updates or fallback suggestions), and a small acceptance harness (screenshots + Percy/Chromatic notes) for follow-up regression checks.

title: Migrate legacy interaction tokens and audit keyboard landmarks for assistive mode
preview: Phase 1 effort to migrate deprecated interaction tokens used across buttons, form controls, table rows, and chart legends; perform a focused keyboard-landmarks audit for the new assistive/compact mode; and deliver conformance artifacts and a lightweight VPAT-style package for product teams.
text: Phase 1 effort to migrate deprecated interaction tokens used across buttons, form controls, table rows, and chart legends; perform a focused keyboard-landmarks audit for the new assistive/compact mode; and deliver conformance artifacts and a lightweight VPAT-style package for product teams.

title: Cluster Guardian: health-orbit canvas & burst orchestration for dedicated fleets
preview: Design a consolidated 'Cluster Guardian' canvas for Dedicated capacity that surfaces real-time health signals, predictive stress windows, and an orchestration panel for temporary burst allocations. The goal is to give SREs and platform engineers a single surface to assess fleet resiliency, tune autoscaling policies,...
text: Design a consolidated 'Cluster Guardian' canvas for Dedicated capacity that surfaces real-time health signals, predictive stress windows, and an orchestration panel for temporary burst allocations. The goal is to give SREs and platform engineers a single surface to assess fleet resiliency, tune autoscaling policies, and authorize short-lived burst budgets with clear cost and latency trade-offs. Deliverables: high-fidelity Figma prototype, interaction spec for rollouts & fallback, a small component library (status orb, ledger rows, resilience lanes), and a handoff checklist for engineering and product. Focus areas: (1) health-orbit visualization summarizing per-pool KPIs without overwhelming rows of metrics, (2) an autoscaling policy composer that previews expected throughput/latency under selected rules, (3) a burst orchestration flow including request, approve, and rollback with cost...

title: Failure Polygraph: consolidated failure forensics & retry composer
preview: Design a unified investigative surface inside Redwood Console that combines a failed-request timeline, payload inspection pane, retry composer, and error taxonomy lens. The goal is to reduce mean-time-to-resolution for production failures by giving engineers a single place to triage, compose deterministic retries, a...
text: Design a unified investigative surface inside Redwood Console that combines a failed-request timeline, payload inspection pane, retry composer, and error taxonomy lens. The goal is to reduce mean-time-to-resolution for production failures by giving engineers a single place to triage, compose deterministic retries, and attach rollout/rollback context. This prototype should also surface cost impact per failure class and link to rollout state so operators can correlate incidents with rollouts and quota changes.

title: Per-device performance envelopes and model-pin release workflow
preview: Goal: Define per-device 'performance envelopes' (latency P50/P95, token/sec, memory footprints) for A100/H100/L40S/MI300 variants and codify a model version pinning + publish workflow that ensures safe rollout of new performance profiles to Hosted/Dedicated/Private deployments. This ticket produces a reproducible on...
text: Goal: Define per-device 'performance envelopes' (latency P50/P95, token/sec, memory footprints) for A100/H100/L40S/MI300 variants and codify a model version pinning + publish workflow that ensures safe rollout of new performance profiles to Hosted/Dedicated/Private deployments. This ticket produces a reproducible onboarding checklist, a canonical JSON schema for published profiles, and a gated release process that ties perf profile publication to canary rollout steps and telemetry gating. Scope excludes model compression experiments (separate RFC).

title: Tenant-aware fairness simulator for regional scarcity and graceful degradation
preview: Goal: validate that admission control, circuit breakers, and load-shedding policies enforce predictable SLO degradation across tenants when a region experiences progressive GPU capacity loss or dependency silence. We need a reproducible simulator and harness that can: (1) emulate partial region failures (gradual GPU...
text: Goal: validate that admission control, circuit breakers, and load-shedding policies enforce predictable SLO degradation across tenants when a region experiences progressive GPU capacity loss or dependency silence. We need a reproducible simulator and harness that can: (1) emulate partial region failures (gradual GPU eviction, abrupt node loss, increasing enqueue delays), (2) emulate downstream dependency silence/muting (redis/cache/kv proxies returning timeouts or dropped responses without raising downstream alerts), and (3) measure per-tenant SLOs and fairness metrics under different admission/policy configurations. The output will inform runtime default policies and provide test cases for canary gating of autoscaling and admission improvements.

title: Incident Handoff Drawer and Postmortem Shortcuts for Alerts
preview: Design a compact, persistent 'Handoff Drawer' that surfaces SLO context, on-call assignment, and one-click postmortem links within the Alerts timeline and alert setup flow. Goal: reduce friction during incident transfers and ensure runbooks/postmortem artifacts are discoverable from the alert UI. The drawer should w...
text: Design a compact, persistent 'Handoff Drawer' that surfaces SLO context, on-call assignment, and one-click postmortem links within the Alerts timeline and alert setup flow. Goal: reduce friction during incident transfers and ensure runbooks/postmortem artifacts are discoverable from the alert UI. The drawer should work in both desktop and narrow-width tablet layouts and support keyboard navigation and screen readers.

title: Token Spend Explorer — Role-aware Attribution & Pricing Presets
preview: Goal: Design a Token Spend Explorer UX that gives billing owners and engineers a single-pane view to understand token-level spend across models, routes, and users, and to export cost-attribution results. The view must support role-aware defaults (Billing Admin vs Engineering Lead), pricing presets (flat-rate, slabed...
text:
Goal: Design a Token Spend Explorer UX that gives billing owners and engineers a single-pane view to understand token-level spend across models, routes, and users, and to export cost-attribution results. The view must support role-aware defaults (Billing Admin vs Engineering Lead), pricing presets (flat-rate, slabed-tiers, custom per-model overrides), and an export wizard that produces a reconciliable CSV for accounting.

Scope:
- Main explorer surface: filter bar (time range, model, route, project tag, user role), column composer (tokens, prompt tokens, completion tokens, cost, cost-per-1k, cache-savings estimate), and grouped breakdown cards (by route, by model, by tag).
- Attribution modes: direct token attribution (by request), proportional attribution (split across route components), and cohort-level allocation (by project or customer tag).
- Pricing presets: built-in templates (...

title: Create compact core token set for embedded widgets and dense UIs
preview:
Summary:
Create a compact variant of Redwood's core design tokens to support embedded widgets and dense data surfaces (small charts, table rows, alert banners inside third-party apps). This work introduces a namespaced compact token set (e.g. token-compact/*) that maps to existing tokens via aliases, updates the typ...
text:
Summary:
Create a compact variant of Redwood's core design tokens to support embedded widgets and dense data surfaces (small charts, table rows, alert banners inside third-party apps). This work introduces a namespaced compact token set (e.g. token-compact/*) that maps to existing tokens via aliases, updates the type scale for small contexts, tightens spacing and radius tokens, and flattens shadow/elevation for low-profile surfaces.

Problem / motivation:
Embedded widgets often render within host UIs with constrained vertical space. Using the default token set causes visual bloat and inconsistent alignment with host apps. We need a formally supported compact token set so product teams can opt into denser layouts without bespoke overrides. This also reduces the number of one-off component overrides engineers maintain.

Scope (what's in):
- Define compact variants for color palette keys...

title: Compact Alert Composer Canvas — Guided Rules, Handoff Shortcuts, and Cost Footprint Ribbon
preview: Design a condensed Alert Composer canvas that surfaces three primary dimensions in a single, scannable strip: (1) guided rule creation (thresholds, noisy suppression heuristics), (2) on-call handoff quicklinks (postmortem templates, responder selection, runbook pinning), and (3) an inline cost footprint ribbon that ...
text: Design a condensed Alert Composer canvas that surfaces three primary dimensions in a single, scannable strip: (1) guided rule creation (thresholds, noisy suppression heuristics), (2) on-call handoff quicklinks (postmortem templates, responder selection, runbook pinning), and (3) an inline cost footprint ribbon that estimates token/compute cost impact and suggests cost-savvy mitigation (aggregation window, sampling, dedup). The goal is to reduce time-to-alert creation for platform users, reduce noisy alerts by encouraging sensible defaults, and make cost tradeoffs explicit at creation time.

title: Pinned version I/O covenant and regression protocol
preview: Define and publish a machine-readable covenant for pinned model versions that guarantees I/O shape, parameter aliases, and a published performance signature. The covenant will be enforced by onboarding checks and the regression pipeline. This ticket scopes the contract format, tolerated deltas, validation harness, a...
text: Define and publish a machine-readable covenant for pinned model versions that guarantees I/O shape, parameter aliases, and a published performance signature. The covenant will be enforced by onboarding checks and the regression pipeline. This ticket scopes the contract format, tolerated deltas, validation harness, and the publishing flow to the model catalog. Goal: give product teams a clear SLA-like promise for pinned models that balances strictness (prevent regressions) with pragmatic adapters (parameter aliasing, safe canonicalizers).
